DataSources.description: List of data sources that can be used in the rest of the execution. They provide data that can be sent to the tested system or used by the execution itself to perform a multitude of logics.

Name.description: Name of data source to reference it by (must be unique)

Generator.description: The name of the generator to use

Lazy.description: True to iterate over data lazily

DataSourceNames.description: Names of data sources to pass to this data source for usage, those data sources dont have to be defined before this data source.

DataSourcePatterns.description: Regex patterns of data sources to pass to this data source for usage, those data sources dont have to be defined before this data source.

GeneratorConfiguration.description: Implementation configuration for the generator, the configuration given here is loaded into the provided generator dynamically.

Serialize.description: Serialize to use on the generated data

Serializer.description: The serializer type to use for serializing. Null means no serialization will happen. Options are all available `QaaS.Framework.Serialization` serializers

Deserialize.description: Deserialize to use on the generated data

Deserializer.description: The deserializer type to use for deserializing. Null means no deserialization will happen. Options are all available `QaaS.Framework.Serialization` deserializers

SpecificType.description: Configuration for making deserializer deserialize into a specific C# object, if set to null will deserialize to default deserilizer's C# object

AssemblyName.description: The name of the assembly the type is located in, If no value is given by default tries to take the entry assembly

TypeFullName.description: The full name (including path) of the type

anyOf.title: FromCSV
anyOf.description: Retrieves CSV rows from files in a configured path in the file system. `DataSources`: Not used. `SessionData`: Not used.

Generator.description: The name of the generator to use

GeneratorConfiguration.title: FromCSV
GeneratorConfiguration.description: Retrieves CSV rows from files in a configured path in the file system. `DataSources`: Not used. `SessionData`: Not used.

Path.description: The path of the directory containing the relevant files

SearchPattern.description: The search string to match against the names of files in path. This parameter can contain a combination of valid literal path and wildcard (* and ?) characters, but it doesn't support regular expressions.

DataArrangeOrder.description: In which order to arrange the loaded data. Arranges data based of the key identifier of the item.  Options: [ `AsciiAsc` - orders by the ascii value ascending / `AsciiDesc` - orders by the ascii value descending / `FirstNumericalAsc` - orders by the first found numerical value in the string ascending /`FirstNumericalDesc` - orders by the first found numerical value in the string descending /'Unordered' - does not order the data ]

Count.description: The number of items to generate out of the given data sources (If count is bigger than available number of items an exception will be thrown), if no value is given generates the number of items in the given data sources

DataUuidRegexExpression.description: A regex expression to use for filtering data from the external source by their UUID, only items that match the regex will be taken

StorageMetaData.description: The way to store the name of the generated data as key in StorageMetaData. Options:[ `FullPath` - stores the full path of the item as the key / `RelativePath` - stores the relative path related to the prefix / `ItemName` - stores only the name of the item as the key / `None` - does not store the name of the item

Delimiter.description: The delimiter used between columns in the CSV content.

HasHeaderRecord.description: True when the first non-empty row contains the column names.

ColumnNames.description: Column names to use when `HasHeaderRecord` is false. If omitted, names are auto-generated as `Column1`, `Column2`, and so on.

SkipEmptyRows.description: True to skip rows whose fields are all empty or whitespace.

TrimWhiteSpace.description: True to trim surrounding whitespace from parsed fields.

anyOf.title: FromDataLake
anyOf.description: Supports generating data from data lake. `DataSources`: Not used. `SessionData`: Not used.

Generator.description: The name of the generator to use

GeneratorConfiguration.title: FromDataLake
GeneratorConfiguration.description: Supports generating data from data lake. `DataSources`: Not used. `SessionData`: Not used.

TrinoServerUri.description: The trino server to connnect to

Username.description: The username for connecting to the trino server

Password.description: The password for connecting to the trino server

ClientTag.description: The client tag to use for connection

Query.description: The query to execute against the datalake

ColumnsToIgnore.description: The columns to ignore in the query results, if no columns are given doesn't ignore any columns

anyOf.title: FromDataSources
anyOf.description: Supports generating data from multiple DataSources that contain data. `DataSources`: Used. `SessionData`: Passed to used DataSources.

Generator.description: The name of the generator to use

GeneratorConfiguration.title: FromDataSources
GeneratorConfiguration.description: Supports generating data from multiple DataSources that contain data. `DataSources`: Used. `SessionData`: Passed to used DataSources.

Count.description: The number of items to generate out of the given data sources (If count is bigger than available number of items an exception will be thrown), if no value is given generates the number of items in the given data sources

anyOf.title: FromFileSystem
anyOf.description: Retrieves data from a configured path to a directory in the file system. `DataSources`: Not used. `SessionData`: Not used.

Generator.description: The name of the generator to use

GeneratorConfiguration.description: Retrieves data from a configured path to a directory in the file system. `DataSources`: Not used. `SessionData`: Not used.

DataArrangeOrder.description: In which order to arrange the loaded data. Arranges data based of the key identifier of the item.  Options: [ `AsciiAsc` - orders by the ascii value ascending / `AsciiDesc` - orders by the ascii value descending / `FirstNumericalAsc` - orders by the first found numerical value in the string ascending /`FirstNumericalDesc` - orders by the first found numerical value in the string descending /'Unordered' - does not order the data ]

Count.description: The number of items to generate out of the given data sources (If count is bigger than available number of items an exception will be thrown), if no value is given generates the number of items in the given data sources

StorageMetaData.description: The way to store the name of the generated data as key in StorageMetaData. SearchPattern.description: The search string to match against the names of files in path. This parameter can contain a combination of valid literal path and wildcard (* and ?) characters, but it doesn't support regular expressions.

anyOf.title: FromLettuceDataSources
anyOf.description: Supports generating data from multiple DataSources that contain lettuce files. `DataSources`: Used, must all contain Lettuce formatted data deserialized into Json (JsonNode). `SessionData`: Passed to used DataSources.

Generator.description: The name of the generator to use

GeneratorConfiguration.title: FromLettuceDataSources
GeneratorConfiguration.description: Supports generating data from multiple DataSources that contain lettuce files. `DataSources`: Used, must all contain Lettuce formatted data deserialized into Json (JsonNode). `SessionData`: Passed to used DataSources.

Count.description: The number of items to generate out of the given data sources (If count is bigger than available number of items an exception will be thrown), if no value is given generates the number of items in the given data sources

anyOf.title: FromS3
anyOf.description: Retrieves data from a configured S3 bucket. `DataSources`: Not used. `SessionData`: Not used.

Generator.description: The name of the generator to use

GeneratorConfiguration.title: FromS3
GeneratorConfiguration.description: Retrieves data from a configured S3 bucket. `DataSources`: Not used. `SessionData`: Not used.

DataArrangeOrder.description: In which order to arrange the loaded data. Arranges data based of the key identifier of the item.  Options: [ `AsciiAsc` - orders by the ascii value ascending / `AsciiDesc` - orders by the ascii value descending / `FirstNumericalAsc` - orders by the first found numerical value in the string ascending /`FirstNumericalDesc` - orders by the first found numerical value in the string descending /'Unordered' - does not order the data ]

Count.description: The number of items to generate out of the given data sources (If count is bigger than available number of items an exception will be thrown), if no value is given generates the number of items in the given data sources

DataUuidRegexExpression.description: A regex expression to use for filtering data from the external source by their UUID, only items that match the regex will be taken

StorageMetaData.description: The way to store the name of the generated data as key in StorageMetaData. Options:[ `FullPath` - stores the full path of the item as the key / `RelativePath` - stores the relative path related to the prefix / `ItemName` - stores only the name of the item as the key / `None` - does not store the name of the item

LoadMetadataFirst.description: Wheather to load lightweight S3 metadata first and then load objects sequentially (true) or load all objects parallelly (false)

ForcePathStyle.description: When true, requests will always use path style addressing

Delimiter.description: Delimiter of the objects to extract from s3 bucket, this determines what objects will be retrieved from the bucket, objects that have at least one occurence of the delimiter in their relative path after the `Prefix` will not be retrieved from the bucket.

Prefix.description: The prefix of the objects, in the s3 bucket to take

SkipEmptyObjects.description: Whether to skip the loading of empty s3 objects or not, if true skips them if false doesnt skip them

Generator.description: The name of the generator to use

items.title: FromSessionDataDataSourcesConfiguration
items.description: Supports generating data from multiple DataSources that contain SessionData files. `DataSources`: All Used. Must all be serialized (`byte[]`) `SessionData` items. `SessionData`: Passed to used DataSources.

SessionName.description: name of the session to load the outputs/inputs of as part of the generated data

CommunicationDataList.description: All of the communnication data items in the given session data to load as part of the generated data by the order listed here

Type.description: The type of the communication data to generate. Options: [ `Input` -  an input communication data from the Inputs list in the SessionData structure / `Output` - an output communication data from the Outputs list in the SessionData structure ]

Name.description: The name of the communication data in the session data to generate

anyOf.title: Json
anyOf.description: Supports generating data from Json data sources. `DataSources`: Used, should only be a single one and must contain data deserialized into Json (JsonNode). `SessionData`: Passed to used DataSources.

Generator.description: The name of the generator to use

GeneratorConfiguration.title: Json
GeneratorConfiguration.description: Supports generating data from Json data sources. `DataSources`: Used, should only be a single one and must contain data deserialized into Json (JsonNode). `SessionData`: Passed to used DataSources.

Path.description: The path (JSONPath query language Feb 2024, by Stefan Gossner) of the field to inject to

ValueType.description: The type of static field injection to use

Name.description: The name of the data source to use for the value of the field

OutOfRangePolicy.description: The policy to use if the data source is out of range

Year.description: Constant Year of the date time

Month.description: Constant Month of the date time

Day.description: Constant Day of the date time

DayOffset.description: Day offset from the date time

Hour.description: Constant Hour of the date time

HourOffset.description: Hour offset from the date time

Minute.description: Constant Minute of the date time

MinuteOffset.description: Minute offset from the date time

Second.description: Constant Second of the date time

SecondOffset.description: Second offset from the date time

Millisecond.description: Constant Millisecond of the date time

MillisecondOffset.description: Millisecond offset from the date time

TimeZone.description: Timezone of the date time (default UTC)

Format.description: Format of the date time (default ISO 8601 without Tim Zone)

UnixEpochTime.description: The unix epoch time field configuration

anyOf.title: Stacking
anyOf.description: Supports generating data from multiple DataSources by configured items per generator. `DataSources`: Used. `SessionData`: Passed to used DataSources.

Generator.description: The name of the generator to use

GeneratorConfiguration.title: Stacking
GeneratorConfiguration.description: Supports generating data from multiple DataSources by configured items per generator. `DataSources`: Used. `SessionData`: Passed to used DataSources.

Count.description: The number of items to generate out of the given data sources (If count is bigger than available number of items an exception will be thrown), if no value is given generates the number of items in the given data sources

ItemsPerGenerator.description: The number of items to generate out of the given data sources in turn

LoopFinishedGenerators.description: When true, generators will restart when there are no more items in them

anyOf.description: Supports custom hook implementations that are not part of the mirrored common package set.

Generator.description: The name of the generator to use

GeneratorConfiguration.description: Implementation configuration for the generator, the configuration given here is loaded into the provided generator dynamically.

Stubs.description: List of transaction stubs that can be used for server actions.They provide processing functionality to exercise transaction data.

Name.description: Name of data source to reference it by (must be unique)

Processor.description: The name of the processor to use

DataSourceNames.description: Names of data sources to pass to this data source for usage, those data sources dont have to be defined before this data source.

ProcessorConfiguration.description: Implementation configuration for the processor, the configuration given here is loaded into the provided processor dynamically.

RequestBodyDeserialization.description: Deserialize to use on the request body

Deserializer.description: The deserializer type to use for deserializing. Null means no deserialization will happen. Options are all available `QaaS.Framework.Serialization` deserializers

SpecificType.description: Configuration for making deserializer deserialize into a specific C# object, if set to null will deserialize to default deserilizer's C# object

AssemblyName.description: The name of the assembly the type is located in, If no value is given by default tries to take the entry assembly

TypeFullName.description: The full name (including path) of the type

ResponseBodySerialization.description: Serialize to use on the response body

Serializer.description: The serializer type to use for serializing. Null means no serialization will happen. Options are all available `QaaS.Framework.Serialization` serializers

Processor.description: The name of the processor to use

Rules.description: Rules evaluated in order. The first matching rule wins.

ExpectedValue.description: Expected value for the selected request header or path parameter

ResponseBody.description: Response body when the rule matches

StatusCode.description: Response Status Code when the rule matches

ContentType.description: Response Content-Type header when the rule matches

ResponseHeaders.description: Response headers when the rule matches

DefaultBody.description: Fallback response body when no rule matches

Processor.description: The name of the processor to use

DataSourceName.description: Optional data source name. When omitted, the first data source is used.

SelectionMode.description: Selection mode used to pick a generated item from the resolved data source

Index.description: Zero-based item index used when SelectionMode is ByIndex

FallbackBody.description: Fallback body to return when the data source cannot be resolved or selected

Processor.description: The name of the processor to use

BodyPropertyName.description: Name of the JSON property that will contain the request body

IncludeBodyType.description: Include the CLR body type as bodyType in the response

IncludeUri.description: Include the request URI as uri in the response

IncludeRequestHeaders.description: Include request headers as requestHeaders in the response

IncludePathParameters.description: Include path parameters as pathParameters in the response

Processor.description: The name of the processor to use

PreserveMetaData.description: Preserve request metadata and only replace the HTTP response metadata

Processor.description: The name of the processor to use

UseRequestUriAsInstance.description: Use the request URI as the problem details instance when no explicit instance is configured

Processor.description: The name of the processor to use

IncludeRequestHeaders.description: Include request headers in the echoed JSON response

IncludePathParameters.description: Include request path parameters in the echoed JSON response

IncludeUri.description: Include request Uri in the echoed JSON response

Processor.description: The name of the processor to use

Body.description: Response body to return as UTF-8 text

Processor.description: The name of the processor to use
The name of the processor to use
The name of the processor to use

Prefix.description: Text to prepend to the transformed payload

Suffix.description: Text to append to the transformed payload

SearchText.description: Optional text to search for in the incoming payload

ReplacementText.description: Replacement text for SearchText. Null removes the matched text.

TrimWhitespace.description: Trim leading and trailing whitespace before applying prefix, suffix, and replacement

anyOf.description: Supports custom hook implementations that are not part of the mirrored common package set.

Processor.description: The name of the processor to use

ProcessorConfiguration.description: Implementation configuration for the processor, the configuration given here is loaded into the provided processor dynamically.

Server.description: The legacy single server mocker instance to run.

Port.description: The port to expose on the http server

Endpoints.description: All endpints which handled by the http server

Name.description: The http endpoint action name identifier

TransactionStubName.description: The name of the transaction stub to process the request through

IsSecuredSchema.description: To run the server with a secured schema. This is for mainly for local testing.

CertificatePath.description: Server certificate path (.pfx) used when IsSecuredSchema is true

CertificatePassword.description: Server certificate password used when IsSecuredSchema is true

IsLocalhost.description: To run the server host as localhost. This is for mainly for local testing.

NotFoundTransactionStubName.description: Transaction stub referred when unknown action is triggered

InternalErrorTransactionStubName.description: Transaction stub referred when internal error in an action is triggered

ConnectionAcceptanceValue.description: The http connection acceptance value used for the semaphore (Multiplied with local processor count)

Port.description: The port to expose on the grpc server

Services.description: The grpc services and rpc actions that are handled by the mocker

ProtoNamespace.description: The namespace containing generated grpc proto classes

AssemblyName.description: The assembly containing generated grpc proto classes

Actions.description: The rpc actions for this grpc service

TransactionStubName.description: The transaction stub used for this rpc method

IsSecuredSchema.description: To run the server with TLS credentials

IsLocalhost.description: To run the server host as localhost. This is mainly for local testing.

NotFoundTransactionStubName.description: Transaction stub referred when unknown action is triggered

InternalErrorTransactionStubName.description: Transaction stub referred when internal error in an action is triggered

CertificatePath.description: Server certificate path (.pfx) used when IsSecuredSchema is true

CertificatePassword.description: Server certificate password used when IsSecuredSchema is true

BindingIpAddress.description: Subnet ipv4 mask to bind socket client's connection to

ConnectionAcceptanceValue.description: The socket's connection acceptance value used for the semaphore (Multiplied with local processor count)

Endpoints.description: All socket endpoint-implementation which handled by the socket server

Port.description: Port that socket listenes and binds connection to

ProtocolType.description: Specifies the protocol to use in the socket

AddressFamily.description: Specifies the addresses masks to approve connections to

BufferSizeBytes.description: The size of communication messages buffer, in bytes

NagleAlgorithm.description: Whether to use the Nagle Algorithm or not - to reduce small packets and communicate more efficiently over Tcp/Ip connection

LingerTimeSeconds.description: The number of seconds to retain connection after communication. `null` means it won't remain connected

TimeoutMs.description: Timeout in milliseconds for socket to perform method before terminating connection

Action.description: The socket action to perform on the client connection endpoint

Name.description: The socket server action name identifier

Method.description: The socket server method to perform on the client connection

DataSourceName.description: Name of the data-source for the socket server to broadcast data by

TransactionStubName.description: The name of the transaction stub to process the data through

CertificatePath.description: Server certificate path (.pfx) used when IsSecuredSchema is true

CertificatePassword.description: Server certificate password used when IsSecuredSchema is true

BindingIpAddress.description: Subnet ipv4 mask to bind socket client's connection to

ConnectionAcceptanceValue.description: The socket's connection acceptance value used for the semaphore (Multiplied with local processor count)

Endpoints.description: All socket endpoint-implementation which handled by the socket server

Port.description: Port that socket listenes and binds connection to

ProtocolType.description: Specifies the protocol to use in the socket

AddressFamily.description: Specifies the addresses masks to approve connections to

BufferSizeBytes.description: The size of communication messages buffer, in bytes

NagleAlgorithm.description: Whether to use the Nagle Algorithm or not - to reduce small packets and communicate more efficiently over Tcp/Ip connection

LingerTimeSeconds.description: The number of seconds to retain connection after communication. `null` means it won't remain connected

TimeoutMs.description: Timeout in milliseconds for socket to perform method before terminating connection

Action.description: The socket action to perform on the client connection endpoint

Name.description: The socket server action name identifier

Method.description: The socket server method to perform on the client connection

DataSourceName.description: Name of the data-source for the socket server to broadcast data by

TransactionStubName.description: The name of the transaction stub to process the data through

Port.description: The port to expose on the http server

Endpoints.description: All endpints which handled by the http server

Name.description: The http endpoint action name identifier

TransactionStubName.description: The name of the transaction stub to process the request through

IsSecuredSchema.description: To run the server with a secured schema. This is for mainly for local testing.

CertificatePath.description: Server certificate path (.pfx) used when IsSecuredSchema is true

CertificatePassword.description: Server certificate password used when IsSecuredSchema is true

IsLocalhost.description: To run the server host as localhost. This is for mainly for local testing.

NotFoundTransactionStubName.description: Transaction stub referred when unknown action is triggered

InternalErrorTransactionStubName.description: Transaction stub referred when internal error in an action is triggered

ConnectionAcceptanceValue.description: The http connection acceptance value used for the semaphore (Multiplied with local processor count)

Port.description: The port to expose on the grpc server

Services.description: The grpc services and rpc actions that are handled by the mocker

ProtoNamespace.description: The namespace containing generated grpc proto classes

AssemblyName.description: The assembly containing generated grpc proto classes

Actions.description: The rpc actions for this grpc service

TransactionStubName.description: The transaction stub used for this rpc method

IsSecuredSchema.description: To run the server with TLS credentials

IsLocalhost.description: To run the server host as localhost. This is mainly for local testing.

NotFoundTransactionStubName.description: Transaction stub referred when unknown action is triggered

InternalErrorTransactionStubName.description: Transaction stub referred when internal error in an action is triggered

CertificatePath.description: Server certificate path (.pfx) used when IsSecuredSchema is true

CertificatePassword.description: Server certificate password used when IsSecuredSchema is true

BindingIpAddress.description: Subnet ipv4 mask to bind socket client's connection to

ConnectionAcceptanceValue.description: The socket's connection acceptance value used for the semaphore (Multiplied with local processor count)

Endpoints.description: All socket endpoint-implementation which handled by the socket server

Port.description: Port that socket listenes and binds connection to

ProtocolType.description: Specifies the protocol to use in the socket

AddressFamily.description: Specifies the addresses masks to approve connections to

BufferSizeBytes.description: The size of communication messages buffer, in bytes

NagleAlgorithm.description: Whether to use the Nagle Algorithm or not - to reduce small packets and communicate more efficiently over Tcp/Ip connection

LingerTimeSeconds.description: The number of seconds to retain connection after communication. `null` means it won't remain connected

TimeoutMs.description: Timeout in milliseconds for socket to perform method before terminating connection

Action.description: The socket action to perform on the client connection endpoint

Name.description: The socket server action name identifier

Method.description: The socket server method to perform on the client connection

DataSourceName.description: Name of the data-source for the socket server to broadcast data by

TransactionStubName.description: The name of the transaction stub to process the data through

Host.description: Redis hostname (should contain the port too for example: - 'host1:8080')

AbortOnConnectFail.description: If true, connect will not create connection while no servers are available

ConnectRetry.description: The number of times to repeat connect attempts during initial connect

ClientName.description: Identification for the connection within redis

AsyncTimeout.description: Time(ms) to allow for asynchronous operations

Ssl.description: Specifies that SSL encryption should be used

SslHost.description: Enforces a preticular SSL host identity on the server's certificate

KeepAlive.description: Time (seconds) at which to send a message to help keep alive
